    Other Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strike, April 13, 2026 SOUTHCOM 13 Apr 2026 -- Applying total systemic friction on the cartels.
  • Strikes on alleged drug boats kill 5 in eastern Pacific, U.S. military says Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. military said Sunday that it blew up two boats accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing a total of five people and leaving one survivor, as the Trump administration pursues its campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin America while preparing a naval blockade of Iranian ports, according to the Associated Press.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • What To Know About The US Naval Blockade Of Iran RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- A US naval blockade on ships entering Iranian ports and coastal areas has come into effect, following failed peace talks between US and Iranian negotiators over the weekend.
  • April 12: Fragile ceasefire in effect as Iran-US talks in Islamabad end without breakthrough Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- The failure of Iran-US talks in Islamabad on Sunday due to maximalist American demands set the tone for a tense and uncertain day.

  • Trump warns any Iranian ships that come close to US blockade 'will be immediately eliminated' Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- Any Iranian ships that come close to the US blockade of Iranian ports "will be immediately eliminated," Anadolu Agency reports citing US President Donald Trump.
  • Toll Booths and Tankers: How the US Navy and Economic Sanctions Aim To Squeeze Tehran RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- A US naval blockade on ships entering Iranian ports and coastal areas took effect on April 13 as Washington increasingly turns to "economic statecraft" to force Tehran to agree on a peace deal after weeks of air strikes.
  • U.S. forces to begin blockading ships entering or exiting Iranian ports on Monday People's Daily 13 Apr 2026 -- U.S. forces will begin implementing a blockade of "all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports" on Monday at 10 a.m. Eastern Time, U.S. Central Command said in a statement Sunday.
  • Trump Confirms Blockade Of Iranian Ports RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- President Donald Trump has confirmed that the US will start blockading ships from entering or exiting Iranian ports from 10 a.m. Eastern Time (5.30 p.m. in Iran) on April 13.
  • Trump announces Strait of Hormuz naval blockade after failing to reach deal with Iran People's Daily 13 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump announced on Sunday that the U.S. Navy would begin blocking ships trying to enter or leave the Strait of Hormuz, after peace talks with Iran failed to produce a deal.

  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at the Government Meeting Israel - Prime Minister 13 Apr 2026 -- "I spoke yesterday with Vice President JD Vance. He called me from his plane on the way back from Islamabad. He briefed me in detail, as members of this administration do daily, on the progress of the negotiations; in this case, the collapse of the negotiations."

  • Iran Warns No Gulf Ports Will Be Safe If US Begins Blockade RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- On April 13, the Khatam al-Anbiya headquarters, which is the central operational headquarters of Iran's armed forces, said that if the announced US naval blockade of Iranian ports was implemented, "no port in the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman will be safe."
  • Pezeshkian: Iran will only continue talks within framework of international law Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has firmly reiterated that Iran will continue all negotiations exclusively within the framework of international law and regulations, while underscoring Tehran's firm commitment to diplomacy, regional stability and the protection of its national rights in the face of the US-Israeli war of aggression.
  • Iran FM: US 'excessive demands' hindered deal in Islamabad talks Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- The Iranian foreign minister says Washington's "excessive demands" hampered an agreement during the negotiations in Pakistan aimed at a conclusive end to the US-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran will surprise aggressors with new tactics and capabilities if war resumes: IRGC Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) warns that the Iranian armed forces did not use their full capacity in their reprisal missile and drone attacks against US and Israeli assets across the region, and will unveil new capabilities that are far beyond the enemy's imagination in the event of renewed aggression.
  • Iran on blockade of Hormuz: US can't win 'war of choice' through 'revenge of choice' Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Iran has condemned the US attempt to impose a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, questioning whether a "revenge of choice" against the global economy can be an option to win an unlawful "war of choice."
  • Iran restores key rail links after damage caused by US-Israeli assaults Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Iran has restored train services in East Azarbaijan Province after repairing the damage caused by US-Israeli strikes, says the director general of railways in the northwestern Iranian province.
  • Iran warns any attack on its ports will make every Persian Gulf port insecure Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Iran's military has warned that any threat to the country's ports would trigger a broader regional response, declaring that no port in the Persian Gulf or the Sea of Oman would remain secure if Iranian ports are targeted.
  • US, Israel will retreat from region empty-handed: IRGC Quds Force chief Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- The chief commander of the Quds Force of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says the United States and the Israeli regime will eventually retreat from the West Asia region without having obtained any achievement.
  • 'Ridiculous': Iran army's navy chief mocks Trump threats to block Hormuz strait Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Navy commander has dismissed President Donald Trump's latest threats to impose a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz as "very ridiculous and laughable," saying the Iranian military is closely monitoring every move of the US fleet in the region.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the Middle East UN Secretary-General 13 Apr 2026
  • US-Iran war: Guterres calls for continued talks, end to ceasefire violations UN News 13 Apr 2026 -- The United States and Iran must continue talks aimed at ending their weeks-long war, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said on Monday, underscoring that there is no military solution to the conflict.
  • 'Clock is ticking': Hormuz disruption raises fears of global food crisis UN News 13 Apr 2026 -- The clock is ticking for global food systems as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz threaten to choke off the flow of fuel and crucial fertilizers needed for the next planting season - also raising the risk of higher food prices and a new wave of inflation.
  • FAO: Protracted Strait of Hormuz crisis could turn into global agrifood catastrophe FAO 13 Apr 2026 -- Ships carrying critical agricultural inputs must start moving through the Strait of Hormuz as soon as possible to ward off the risks of a dangerous spike in food price inflation later this year that could trigger a cascade of effects similar to the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic crisis, according to the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO).

  • China says end of US hostilities against Iran only solution to Hormuz crisis Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- China has called for an immediate end to hostilities as the only viable path to resolving tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Strait of Hormuz faces volatility amid US President's blockade order; Chinese FM notes root cause is military conflict, urges restraint Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- As the US blockade of Strait of Hormuz came into effect on Monday 10:00 A.M. ET., the US military said it will block all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports, according to Al Jazeera.
  • Strait of Hormuz blockade not in common interests of int'l community: China's top diplomat Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- China's top diplomat Wang Yi on Monday said blocking the Strait of Hormuz is not in the common interests of the international community, and achieving a comprehensive and lasting ceasefire through political and diplomatic means is the fundamental way to resolve the issue.
  • China ready to further play constructive role regarding Iran conflict: Premier Li Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Premier Li Qiang said on Monday that China is ready to further play a constructive role and contribute to the restoration of peace and tranquility in the Gulf region.
  • Chinese FM meets with special envoy of UAE president, calls for political and diplomatic solution to blockade of Strait of Hormuz Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- China fully understands the legitimate security concerns of Gulf Arab states and supports the United Arab Emirates in safeguarding its sovereignty, security and legitimate rights and interests, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Monday during a meeting in Beijing with Khaldoon Khalifa Al Mubarak, special envoy of the president of the United Arab Emirates (UAE), who accompanied Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates (UAE), on his visit to China.
  • US president orders military blockade of Strait of Hormuz; Chinese FM says the way to resolve the issue of the Strait is to achieve a ceasefire, end hostilities Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- The root cause of the disruption at the Strait of Hormuz is the military conflict. To solve the issue, the conflict must stop as soon as possible. All parties need to remain calm and exercise restraint. China will continue playing a constructive role, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday when asked to comment on US President Donald Trump's order on Sunday that the US Navy would start blockading the Strait of Hormuz, as well as he called on China to buy oil from the US or Venezuela, and China's view on security in the Strait and related energy issues.
  • Chinese FM expresses opposition toward groundless smears and malicious associations over reports claiming China preparing to provide weapons to Iran Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- China has always adopted a prudent and responsible approach to arms exports, implementing strict controls in accordance with its domestic export control laws and regulations as well as its international obligations, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday in response to a question about reports claiming that "China is preparing to provide weapons to Iran" and that "China has supplied Iran with dual-use technologies and related components."

  • Russia warns Trump's threats against Strait of Hormuz will roil global markets Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Russia has warned of "negative" consequences for global markets following US President Donald Trump's threat to impose a blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Foreign Minister Discusses US-Iran Talks in Call with Iranian Counterpart SPA 13 Apr 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah received a phone call today from Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi
  • Saudi, Iranian foreign ministers discuss regional developments Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- Saudi Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan and his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi, discussed the latest regional developments during a phone call on Monday, Anadolu Agency reports citing the Saudi Foreign Ministry.

  • Starmer says UK will not join Trump's blockade of Iran's ports Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- The UK will not be involved in enforcing the US military blockade of Iran, BBC reports citing Sir Keir Starmer.
  • NATO allies refuse to participate in US blockade of Strait of Hormuz Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- The United States' NATO allies have refused to take part in US President Donald Trump's plan to blockade the Strait of Hormuz, as Iran warns Washington and its partners against any miscalculation in the Persian Gulf.
  • Japan-Pakistan Summit Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 13 Apr 2026
  • Philippines Convenes Second Special ASEAN Foreign Ministers' Meeting on the Middle East Situation Philippines DFA 13 Apr 2026 -- Secretary of Foreign Affairs Ma. Theresa P. Lazaro, in her capacity as Chair of the ASEAN Foreign Ministers' Meeting, convened a virtual Second Special ASEAN Foreign Ministers' Meeting on 13 April 2026 to address the ongoing crisis in the Middle East and its implications for regional economic security and stability.
  • DFA pursues safe passage for PH vessels amid Strait of Hormuz tensions Philippine News Agency 13 Apr 2026 -- Rising tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and disruptions in global shipping have prompted efforts to secure the passage of Philippine vessels through the critical oil route.
  • Palace wary as US-Iran talks collapse, oil risks persist Philippine News Agency 13 Apr 2026 -- Malacañang on Monday expressed concern over the failure of ceasefire talks between the United States (U.S.) and Iran, warning of continued risks to global oil supply and prices.
  • ASEAN backs US-Iran ceasefire, urges maritime safety, continued talks Philippine News Agency 13 Apr 2026 -- Foreign ministers from the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) on Monday underscored the importance of the two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran for maritime safety and security.
  • Yemen vows military escalation if US, Israel resume war on Iran Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Yemen's Foreign Ministry says the Arab country's position remains steadfast in active participation and escalation of military operations if the United States and the Israeli regime resume their war of aggression against Iran and the Axis of Resistance.
  • Finnish President Says Iran Blockade 'Makes Sense' From Washington's Perspective, Warns Of Risks RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- Finnish President Alexander Stubb says that a US blockade strategy toward Iran "makes sense" from Washington's perspective, while warning of significant uncertainties and escalation risks.
  • Bahrain summons Iraqi charge d'affaires over 'continued drone attacks' on Gulf states Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- Bahrain on Monday summoned the charge d'affaires of the Iraqi Embassy over "continued drone attacks" targeting the kingdom and other Gulf states from Iraqi territory, Anadolu Agency reports.
  • Australia has not received request to join Trump's blockade of Strait of Hormuz: Albanese Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- Australia has not been asked to participate in US President Donald Trump's blockade of Iran's ports in the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Monday as he urged Washington and Tehran to resume peace talks, Anadolu Agency reports citing to local media.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Secretary of War Hegseth Hosted Bilateral Meeting With Indonesian Defense Minister Sjafrie DoD 13 Apr 2026 -- SECRETARY OF WAR PETE HEGSETH: Well, Minister Sjafrie, welcome to the Pentagon. Welcome. It's an honor to have you. It's an honor to host your team, as well. I learned as we were walking up - last in Washington in 1986 - was training with US forces out of Fort Benning and previous before that, Fort Bragg. So it's great to have you here, sir, to have a soldier here.
  • Readout of Secretary of War Pete Hegseth's Meeting with Indonesian Minister of Defense Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin DoD 13 Apr 2026
  • Hegseth, Indonesian Counterpart Announce Defense Partnership DoD News 13 Apr 2026 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth welcomed his Indonesian counterpart — Defense Minister Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin — to the Pentagon today, where the two men announced the establishment of the Major Defense Cooperation Partnership between the two countries.
  • US Army, industry partner open new artillery assembly facility Army News 13 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Army and Day & Zimmerman opened a new facility in Parsons dedicated to producing 155mm M795 artillery projectiles. The ribbon cutting ceremony, hosted by Maj. Gen. John T. Reim, Portfolio Acquisition Executive for Agile Sustainment and Ammunition, marked a critical step in expanding 155mm artillery production capacity further enhancing the nation's Defense Industrial Base.
  • US, Chilean Air Force crews complete historic refueling US Air Force 13 Apr 2026 -- U.S. and Chilean Air Force (FACh) crews demonstrated advanced interoperability April 4, executing the first-ever Chilean KC-135 Stratotanker air-to-air refueling of two U.S. F-35A Lightning II aircraft — marking a significant step forward in bilateral airpower integration.
  • Flintlock 26 Commences in Côte d'Ivoire and Libya AFRICOM 13 Apr 2026 -- Exercise Flintlock 2026, U.S. Africa Command's premier special operations exercise, officially began with dual opening ceremonies in Libya and Côte d'Ivoire on April 14, 2026.

  • NATO and Azerbaijan strengthen cooperation on the modernisation of defence education NATO 13 Apr 2026 -- From 31 March to 2 April 2026, representatives from NATO's Defence and Security Cooperation Directorate met with the leadership and personnel of Azerbaijan's National Defence University and its affiliated military education institutions in Baku. The visit aimed to assess ongoing reform efforts within the framework of NATO's Defence Education Enhancement Programme (DEEP).
  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on April 6-12, 2026 Lithuania MND 13 Apr 2026

  • For the First Time in Its History, Ukraine Is Armed Sufficiently to Defend Against Russian Strikes - President President of Ukraine 13 Apr 2026
  • Demining Coalition secures additional €2 million for Ukraine Ukraine MOD 13 Apr 2026 -- One of the countries has made an additional €2 million contribution to the Demining Capability Coalition fund for Ukraine.
  • Yulia Svyrydenko: Over 50% of weapons on the frontline are Ukrainian-made Ukraine Government 13 Apr 2026 -- Ukrainian arms manufacturers are a community of brave entrepreneurs who, in partnership with the Defence Forces, create unique technologies.

  • MND surprised by lawmaker suggestion to help U.S. clear mines CNA 13 Apr 2026 -- The Ministry of National Defense (MND) seemed surprised Monday by a Democratic Progressive Party lawmaker's suggestion that Taiwan's military could support the United States in mine-clearing operations in the Strait of Hormuz.

  • First Australian-Made Missiles Test-Fired Australia DOD 13 Apr 2026 -- The first Australian-made Guided Multiple Launch Rocket System (GMLRS) missiles have been successfully test-fired at the Woomera Test Range in South Australia.

  • DRDO to showcase cutting edge tech & Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiatives during mega exhibition at Motihari, Bihar India PIB 13 Apr 2026 -- Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) will display its cutting edge technologies, advance systems, and the robust strides taken towards 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' during a mega exhibition at Mahatma Gandhi Prekshagrih in Motihari, Bihar from April 15 to 18, 2026. Member of Parliament and Chairman, Parliamentary Standing Committee on Defence Shri Radha Mohan Singh will inaugurate the exhibition, on the theme 'Shanti, Satya Aur Vigyan Ka Sangam - Surakshit Aur Aatmanirbhar Bharat Ki Aur'.

  • 50 million boost to defence innovation in South Yorkshire with new Defence Growth Deal UK MOD 13 Apr 2026 -- New £50 million South Yorkshire Defence Growth Deal expanding cutting-edge defence research to help defence companies and boost skills in a region that already supports 3,200 defence jobs.

  • Joint statement by members of the Non-Proliferation and Disarmament Initiative, Towards the 11th Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Review Conference Australia DFAT 13 Apr 2026

  • Navy team destroys floating drone near Sinemorets Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- A specialized team from the Bulgarian Navy destroyed a floating drone in a controlled explosion after it was found on a beach near Sinemorets, the Bulgarian News Agency (BTA) reported citing the Ministry of Defence.

  • Partnership for security: Poland and Norway for regional stability Poland - Ministry of National Defence 13 Apr 2026 -- - Polish-Norwegian relations have never been as good as they are today - they are of the highest quality. It is already a strategic partnership. We have been discussing key issues for the security of eastern and northern flanks. We have been talking about challenges tied to the threat from the Russian Federation, its neo-imperial policy, and all that we face because of the ongoing hybrid war. For a number of years, Poland has been facing attacks on critical infrastructure, airspace violations and incidents on the Baltic. I thank the Minister for joining our strategy to build new security architecture on the Baltic Sea - said the Deputy Prime Minister during a visit to Norway.

  • Call-up system to be extended to whole age group - three municipalities to pilot information days in autumn Finnish MoD 13 Apr 2026 -- The Finnish call-up process will have two parts in the future. The first part will be a call-up information day for the whole age group, providing young people with more information about homeland defence. The second part, the call-up day proper, will be organised later for men liable for military service and for women applying for voluntary military service.

  • PH, Australia, US hold 16th joint drill in WPS Philippine News Agency 13 Apr 2026 -- Philippine, Australian and U.S. air and naval forces conducted the 16th multilateral maritime cooperative activity (MMCA) in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) from April 9 to 12, the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said on Monday.

  • Pope Leo pledges to continue to speak out against war after Trump attack Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Pope Leo XIV says he will continue to speak out against war and suffering after US President Donald Trump said he was not "a fan of the pope" over the pontiff's condemnation of the US-Israeli aggression against Iran.
  • Pope Responds To Trump Remarks, Says He Will 'Continue To Speak Out' RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- Pope Leo has said that he intends to continue taking a stand against war, following criticism by US President Donald Trump on the leader of the 1.4-billion-member church.
  • Trump criticizes Pope Leo over foreign policy stance, says he "should get his act together" Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump criticized Pope Leo XIV in a series of public remarks following the pontiff's recent statements on global conflicts.

  • HE GCCSG Receives Phone Call from US Deputy Secretary of State to Discuss Regional Developments and Strategic Cooperation Gulf Cooperation Council 13 Apr 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Finnish Foreign Minister Valtonen US Dept. of State 13 Apr 2026

  • Netanyahu Visits Southern Lebanon, Says 'The War Continues' RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited southern Lebanon on April 12 and said military operations against the Iran-allied Hezbollah militia would go on despite international calls for a cease-fire.

  • Hezbollah chief: 'Lebanese resistance will not surrender, the battlefield will speak' Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Hezbollah Secretary General Sheikh Naim Qassem has said that the Lebanese Islamic resistance will confront the ongoing Israeli-American aggression against the people of Lebanon and demanded immediate implementation of the November 2024 ceasefire agreement.
  • April 13: Hezbollah's operations against Zionist regime in response to truce breach Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Shortly after Iran and the US announced a ceasefire ending 40 days of aggression against the Islamic Republic, the Israeli regime violated the truce on Wednesday morning by launching devastating strikes on civilian areas across Lebanon.
  • Hezbollah Opposes Direct Lebanon-Israel Talks - Spokesman Sputnik 13 Apr 2026 -- The Hezbollah movement opposes direct talks between Lebanon and Israel and does not expect positive results from them, Mahmoud Komati, deputy head of Hezbollah's political council, told Sputnik.
  • Hezbollah says hit 43 Israeli targets as part of anti-aggression ops on Sunday Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Hezbollah's Islamic Resistance announced on Sunday that it carried out 43 military operations against Israeli enemy positions and troop concentrations along the border and deep inside occupied Palestine, in response to repeated ceasefire violations and the regime's relentless aggression on Lebanese villages.

  • UN experts call for end to attacks on Gaza shelters, forced displacement in West Bank OHCHR 13 Apr 2026 -- UN experts* today expressed grave concern over Israeli military attacks affecting sites sheltering displaced Palestinians in the western Gaza City, alongside renewed patterns of forced displacement in the West Bank, including East Jerusalem.

  • Russia Could Play Key Role in Resolving Middle East Conflict - Hezbollah Spokesman Sputnik 13 Apr 2026 -- Russia, due to its relations with the Islamic Republic of Iran and its strong ties with many Arab countries and Lebanon, could play an important role in the region, Mahmoud Kamati, deputy head of Hezbollah's political council, told Sputnik.

  • Statement by Principals of the Inter-Agency Standing Committee UNICEF 13 Apr 2026 -- End impunity for violations of rules of war

  • PLA Southern Theater Command Navy conducts routine patrol in South China Sea PRC MND 13 Apr 2026
  • Philippine side staging of so-called 'cyanide stunt' has no credibility: FM spokesperson Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- "There is no credibility whatsoever to their story," Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday in response to a question about the Philippines' National Security Council claimed at a briefing that cyanide was found in bottles recovered from Chinese boats near Ren'ai Jiao in February and October 2025.
  • The Philippine side staging so-called 'cyanide farce' lacking in credibility, not worth refuting: FM spokesperson Global Times 13 Apr 2026
  • PLA Southern Theater Command conducts patrols in S.China Sea, will defend rights and interests: spokesperson on Philippines' 'joint patrol' with extraterritorial countries Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- From April 9 to 12, the Navy of the Chinese People's Liberation Army Southern Theater Command conducted routine patrols in the South China Sea, Senior Captain Zhai Shichen, spokesperson for the PLA Southern Theater Command, said in a release published by the theater command on Monday.

  • Sudan: On third anniversary of conflict, UN and ACHPR-AU Fact-Finding Missions jointly warn of escalating violence and heightened risk of further atrocity crimes OHCHR 13 Apr 2026 -- BUG PATTERN NOT FOUND
  • Sudan: Top UN aid official warns of 'abandoned crisis' as war enters fourth year UN News 13 Apr 2026 -- As Sudan's war moves into a fourth year, civilians are still being killed, displaced and subjected to widespread sexual violence, the UN's top humanitarian official in the country warned on Monday, calling for urgent action to stop the fighting and protect civilians.
  • Millions of people in Sudan surviving on one meal a day as food crisis deepens, NGOs say Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- Millions of people in Sudan are surviving on just one meal a day, as the country's food crisis deepens and threatens to spread, Reuters reported citing to a report published on Monday by a group of non-governmental organisations.

  • Army 'anxious' over Reds' recruitment of Fil-Am youths Philippine News Agency 13 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Army's 2nd Infantry Division (2ID) on Monday reiterated the concern of U.S.-based Filipino-Americans Against Communist Terrorism (FACT) following reports of recruitment of Filipino American youths to individuals linked to the Communist Party of the Philippines-New People's Army (CPP-NPA).

News Reports

  • Europe Looks For Reset With Hungary After Magyar's Stunning Victory RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- Just hours after election results showed opposition leader Peter Magyar had scored a landslide victory to end the rule of Viktor Orban and his right-wing Fidesz party, the man slated to become Hungary's next prime minister said there's "no time to waste" in forming a new government.
  • Orban concedes defeat in election under global attention amid speculations of sharp policy shift Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- "The election result is painful for us, but clear," Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban said at the Fidesz party campaign offices, conceding defeat on local time Sunday in a major election loss - signaling the imminent end of his 16 consecutive years in power, as the Xinhua News Agency reported - as results showed Peter Magyar's center-right Tisza party winning a majority, per media reports.
  • Hungary's Tisza party wins parliamentary elections, defeating Orban's ruling coalition People's Daily 13 Apr 2026 -- Hungary's opposition party Tisza, led by Peter Magyar, defeated Prime Minister Viktor Orban's ruling coalition by securing a majority in Sunday's parliamentary elections, according to preliminary results released by the National Election Office (NEO).
  • Hungary's parliamentary elections: vibrant, but no equal opportunities for contestants Council of Europe 13 Apr 2026 -- Divisive rhetoric from ruling party also noted by international observers
  • Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna: the Hungarian election result gives Europe a new opportunity for unity Estonia MFA 13 Apr 2026 -- According to Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna, the Hungarian people clearly chose the direction of the rule of law and Europe in yesterday's elections, marking a historic moment for both Hungary and Europe as a whole.
  • The President congratulated Péter Magyar on victory in the Hungarian parliamentary elections President of the Republic of Lithuania 13 Apr 2026 -- The President's Communication Group
  • Minister Radosław Sikorski congratulates Tisza on election win and invites Hungary's future top diplomat to Warsaw Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Apr 2026 -- Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Radosław Sikorski spoke on the phone today with Anita Orbán, the incoming foreign minister of Hungary's new government.
  • Hungary's Tisza party wins parliamentary elections, defeating Orban's ruling coalition Global Times 13 Apr 2026 -- Hungary's opposition party Tisza, led by Peter Magyar, defeated Prime Minister Viktor Orban's ruling coalition by securing a majority in Sunday's parliamentary elections, according to preliminary results released by the National Election Office (NEO).
  • Hungary's Tisza party wins parliamentary elections as 98.13 pct votes counted: NEO People's Daily 13 Apr 2026 -- Hungary's opposition party Tisza, led by Peter Magyar, defeated Prime Minister Viktor Orban's ruling coalition by securing a majority in Sunday's parliamentary elections, according to preliminary results released by the National Election Office (NEO).

  • DNI Tulsi Gabbard Exposes Conspiracy Used By Congress To Impeach President Trump Director of National Intelligence 13 Apr 2026 -- Today, Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard releases never-before-seen documents exposing a coordinated effort by elements within the Intelligence Community (IC), including a former Inspector General (IG), to manufacture a conspiracy that was used as the basis to impeach President Trump in 2019.

  • Criminal investigation into unmanned aerial vehicle in Iitti continues Police of Finland 13 Apr 2026 -- The criminal investigation into the unmanned aerial vehicle found in Iitti, Päijät-Häme continues. The Border Guard investigates the incident as a suspected territorial violation, and the National Bureau of Investigation investigates a suspected grossly negligent endangerment.

  • Tennessee Man Pleads Guilty to Arson and Attempting to Provide Material Support to a Foreign Terrorist Organization US Dept. of Justice 13 Apr 2026 -- Today, Regan Darby Prater, 28, currently of Tullahoma, Tennessee, entered a guilty plea to one count of arson and one count of attempting to provide material support to a foreign terrorist organization.

  • Russian-Indonesian talks President of Russia 13 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held talks in the Kremlin with President of Indonesia Prabowo Subianto, who is in Russia on a working visit.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's answers to media questions, Moscow, April 13, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Apr 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on April 13, 2026 PRC MofA 13 Apr 2026

  • China's Kinmen, Matsu measures may deepen Taiwan divisions: Scholars CNA 13 Apr 2026 -- Beijing's new economic proposals targeting Kinmen and Matsu merit special attention because they may be aimed at turning the outlying islands into a showcase for cross-Taiwan Strait integration while deepening divisions in Taiwan, scholars said Sunday.
  • KMT chairwoman leaves mainland after 6-day visit People's Daily 13 Apr 2026 -- A delegation led by Cheng Li-wun, chairwoman of the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party, wrapped up its six-day visit to the Chinese mainland and departed Beijing for Taiwan on Sunday afternoon.
  • Mainland unveils package of policies, measures to boost ties with Taiwan People's Daily 13 Apr 2026 -- The Chinese mainland on Sunday rolled out a package of 10 policies and measures -- spanning inter-party communication, infrastructure, travel, trade and culture -- aimed at boosting exchanges and cooperation with Taiwan.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 13 Apr 2026

  • WPK Is Revolutionary Party for People: Rodong Sinmun KCNA 13 Apr 2026 -- The great Workers' Party of Korea is dynamically leading the struggle for great transformation to ensure the long-term state development. Thanks to its ever-victorious leadership, the history of the brilliant dignity, powerful strength and brilliant prosperity, which all the generations of the DPRK have desired, is being given steady continuity on this land, says Rodong Sinmun Monday in an article.

  • FM Sa'ar speaks with Italian Deputy PM and FM Antonio Tajani prior to his departure for Lebanon Israel MFA 13 Apr 2026 -- They discussed, inter aliia, the dialogue between Israel and Lebanon that's expected to begin tomorrow and Israel's continued security operations against Hezbollah.
  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at the Opening Ceremony for Holocaust Martyrs' and Heroes' Remembrance Day Israel - Prime Minister 13 Apr 2026
  • FM Sa'ar addresses the Holocaust Memorial Day Ceremony at the MFA in Jerusalem Israel MFA 13 Apr 2026 -- FM Sa'ar: "We will never let our enemies obtain a nuclear weapon to realize their ambition of our elimination."

  • Fidan: 'Israel cannot live without an enemy' and seeks to declare Turkey as one Press TV 13 Apr 2026 -- Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan says Israel "cannot live without an enemy" and the incumbent cabinet led by Benjamin Netanyahu is now seeking to declare the Ankara government as its new enemy.

  • India - Franco-Indian consultations between Martins Briens, Secretary General of the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs, and Indian Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 13 Apr 2026
  • India-France Foreign Office Consultations (April 13, 2026) India MEA 13 Apr 2026 -- India France Foreign Office Consultations were held on 13 April 2026 in Paris, co-chaired by Foreign Secretary Shri Vikram Misri and Secretary-General of the French Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs, Mr. Martin Briens.

  • Meeting between Jean-Noël Barrot and Norwegian Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 13 Apr 2026
  • The Daily Press Briefing (04/13/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 13 Apr 2026

  • Press conference - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 13 Apr 2026
  • Australia has not received request to join Trump's blockade of Strait of Hormuz: Albanese Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- Australia has not been asked to participate in US President Donald Trump's blockade of Iran's ports in the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Monday as he urged Washington and Tehran to resume peace talks, Anadolu Agency reports citing to local media.

  • Minister Sidhu meets with China's Vice-Premier in charge of financial and economic affairs Canada 13 Apr 2026 -- The Honourable Maninder Sidhu, Minister of International Trade, met today with He Lifeng, Vice-Premier of the People's Republic of China, on the margins of the China International Consumer Products Expo (CICPE) in Haikou.
  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif Prime Minister of Canada 13 Apr 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the Prime Minister of Pakistan, Shehbaz Sharif.

  • Court In Kazakhstan Finds All 19 Defendants Guilty For Anti-China Protests RFE/RL 13 Apr 2026 -- A court in Kazakhstan convicted all 19 defendants in a politically sensitive case heard behind that was linked to an anti-China protest last year near the border between the two countries.

  • United Nations: Speech by High Representative Kaja Kallas at the annual UN Security Council session on EU-UN cooperation European External Action Service (EEAS) 13 Apr 2026 -- Mister President, Excellencies,
  • European Union support for the UN 'more vital than ever', Security Council hears UN News 13 Apr 2026 -- A senior United Nations official on Monday hailed the European Union (EU) as "a major economic and diplomatic actor and a strong advocate of multilateralism."
  • As World Abandons 'Old Rules', UN Cooperation with European Union Needed More Than Ever, High Representative Tells Security Council, amid Calls to Avoid Overreach UN Security Council 13 Apr 2026 -- Conceived as a project of peace in the wake of the Second World War, the European Union today remains one of the United Nations' staunchest and most important regional partners, senior officials from both organizations told the Security Council today, even some delegates sparred over the bloc's influence in an increasingly multipolar and unstable world.

  • OPEC crude production drops 7.9 million bpd in March amid Middle East disruptions Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Apr 2026 -- Crude oil production by the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) fell by around 7.9 million barrels per day (bpd) in March 2026 compared with February, due to disruptions caused by US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran and Iran's retaliatory attacks across the region, Anadolu Agency reports.

  • OIC General Secretariat Participates in Observing the Presidential Elections in the Republic of Djibouti Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 13 Apr 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) dispatched a mission of experts to observe the Presidential elections in the Republic of Djibouti, which took place on Friday, 10 April 2026, following an official invitation to participate in the election observation received by His Excellency the Secretary-General of the Organization

  • The UK supports EU-UN coordination in maintaining international peace and security: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 13 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Cooperation between the UN and the European Union.
